hint.title: Your function likely returns undefined
hint.why: The assertion expected a value but received undefined.
actions: Add an explicit return statement in the main code path.
Verify loops or callbacks are not returning from the wrong scope.
Re-run and check if the first failed test now reports a value mismatch instead.

hint.title: You are reading a property from an undefined value
hint.why: One variable is undefined before property access.
actions: Log/inspect the variable right before the failing access.
Guard nullable values before dereferencing.
Check function arguments and default values on edge inputs.

hint.title: A non-function value is being called
hint.why: A variable expected to be callable is not a function at runtime.
actions: Verify import/export names and default vs named exports.
Check variable shadowing that may overwrite the function reference.
Confirm callback arguments are functions before invoking.

hint.title: Data type mismatch is causing runtime failure
hint.why: The operation expects a different value type than what is passed in.
actions: Print input shapes for failing test cases.
Normalize input types at function boundaries.
Add defensive checks for unexpected types.

hint.title: Boundary condition is likely incorrect
hint.why: The mismatch suggests an index or loop bound issue.
actions: Validate loop start/end conditions and inclusive vs exclusive bounds.
Test with smallest and largest valid indices.
Check whether you should use < or <= for this case.

hint.title: Async flow likely does not match test expectation
hint.why: Promise handling or async return behavior appears inconsistent.
actions: Ensure async functions return/await the expected Promise.
Handle rejection paths explicitly in your implementation.
Avoid mixing callbacks and async/await in one path.

hint.title: A variable is used before being defined
hint.why: Runtime cannot find a referenced identifier.
actions: Check spelling and casing of variable/function names.
Move declarations above first use where needed.
Ensure all branches initialize required values.

hint.title: Output shape/value differs from expectation
hint.why: Your function runs, but produced a different result than expected.
actions: Compare expected vs actual on the first failing test only.
Trace intermediate values that feed the final output.
Confirm mutation order when building arrays/objects.

hint.title: Code has a parse/compile issue
hint.why: The runner cannot execute due to syntax problems.
actions: Check for missing brackets, commas, or stray characters.
Temporarily simplify recent edits to isolate the bad line.
Run once after formatting to catch unbalanced blocks.

hint.title: Execution likely exceeds allowed runtime
hint.why: A loop/recursion path may not terminate quickly enough.
actions: Inspect loops/recursion for missing exit conditions.
Avoid repeated expensive work inside tight loops.
Start by passing the smallest cases, then optimize.

hint.title: State mutation may leak across operations
hint.why: The test likely assumes immutable behavior, but shared state was changed.
actions: Clone input arrays/objects before mutating when required.
Check reused references across iterations.
Verify helper functions do not mutate external state.

hint.title: Edge input handling seems incomplete
hint.why: The failing case likely involves empty, null, or unusual boundary values.
actions: Test empty input, single-item, and extreme values.
Add explicit guards for null/undefined branches.
Ensure output contract is consistent on edge inputs.

fallbackHint.title: Start with the first failing test
fallbackHint.why: The run produced a failure pattern that is not yet mapped to a specific rule.
actions: Read the first failing test name and expected value carefully.
Log intermediate variables right before the failing assertion.
Fix one failure at a time and re-run quickly.
